title: Access-control check allows archived record read to wrong role
bug_description: A read endpoint allows a role that should be denied to view archived records because the archived-state authorization branch is incomplete.
expected_behavior: Only explicitly allowed roles should be able to read archived records, and denied roles should receive an authorization error.
actual_behavior: A disallowed role can still retrieve archived records through the read endpoint.

preconditions: Local API server is running with an archived record fixture
Test credentials exist for both an allowed role and a denied role
steps: Call the read endpoint for an archived record using the denied role credentials
Observe the response status and returned payload
reproduction.expected_behavior: The denied role should receive an authorization failure and no record payload.
reproduction.actual_behavior: The denied role receives the archived record data successfully.

logs: Authorization trace shows the archived-state branch falls back to the general read permission path

manual_checks: Verify denied-role access to archived records is blocked
Verify allowed-role access still succeeds for the same archived record

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if the fix blocks a role that should retain archived-record access.
rollback.notes: Keep the last passing authorization branch available for immediate restore.

scope_notes: Keep the fix inside archived-record authorization checks and the directly affected endpoint tests. Do not broaden into a full permission-system redesign.

notes.root_cause_hint: The archived-state path may bypass the stricter role check and reuse a generic read permission helper.
notes.risk_hint: A broad authorization change could accidentally break normal record reads for allowed roles.
similar_bugs: authorization helper reused in stricter context without additional guard
